name: Code Slop Detection
description: Taste-level review of code for AI-generated patterns in PHP/Laravel and TypeScript/React projects. Catches code that passes every metric but reads like a tutorial — not human-written. Complements technical-debt's quantitative metrics with qualitative review.

categories.name: Comments
categories.description: Narration, empty docblocks, placeholder TODOs, closing-brace labels

categories.name: Naming
categories.description: Generic placeholders, over-descriptive run-ons, suffix abuse, type-in-name

categories.name: Over-engineering
categories.description: Premature interfaces, single-method classes, useless wrappers, dependency creep

categories.name: Defensive overdose
categories.description: Generic catch blocks, impossible null checks, defensive in wrong places

categories.name: Test slop
categories.description: Mock-everything tests, doesn't-throw asserts, mirror-implementation, snapshot abuse

categories.name: Style fingerprints
categories.description: Hyper-consistent formatting, as-any escapes, no HACK scars, debug artifacts, trivial boilerplate

references.title: arXiv 2510.03029 — Investigating the Smells of LLM-Generated Code
